name: VoiceMode
what_it_does: An MCP server and Claude Code plugin that enables natural voice conversations with Claude Code and other MCP-capable AI agents, supporting both cloud (OpenAI) and fully local (Whisper + Kokoro) speech processing with smart silence detection.
use_cases: Hands-free AI coding assistance while walking, cooking, or away from keyboard
Voice-driven development sessions during extended screen-time breaks
Privacy-first local voice interaction using on-device Whisper and Kokoro models
Accessible AI interface for users who prefer or require speech input
not_for: Production voice applications or customer-facing voice bots
Voice interaction with non-MCP AI systems
best_when: You want to talk to Claude Code naturally during development without touching the keyboard, with the option to keep everything fully local for privacy.
avoid_when: You need low-latency, high-accuracy voice for production use cases — dedicated voice platforms like Deepgram or AssemblyAI are better suited.

auth.notes: Optional OpenAI API key for cloud STT/TTS. No auth required for fully local mode using Whisper + Kokoro.

pricing.estimated_workload_costs: Zero for local mode; OpenAI Whisper/TTS API costs apply if cloud mode is used (~$0.006/min for Whisper).
pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Local-only mode is completely free with no external dependencies beyond system audio libraries.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Audio device errors can be opaque; silence detection edge cases not well documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Voice sessions are stateless; each interaction is independent.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires FFmpeg, portaudio, and platform-specific audio libraries installed on host
Local Whisper model download needed on first use (~hundreds of MB)
Silence detection may cut off speech prematurely in noisy environments
WSL on Windows requires additional audio routing configuration
